invariants_list.rule: PASS must be sent immediately following a USER command.
Successful authentication (USER and PASS) is a prerequisite for LIST, RETR, STOR, and NLST commands.
A data connection establishment command (PASV, PORT, EPSV, or EPRT) must precede data transfer commands (LIST, RETR, STOR).
RNTO must follow RNFR to complete a file rename operation.
QUIT command may be issued at any point to terminate the control connection.
After an authentication failure (e.g., 530 response to PASS), the client must re-issue a USER command to retry.
TYPE.type_code: Format: TYPE <type_code> where code is A (ASCII) or I (Binary)

RNTO must follow RNFR to complete a file rename operation.
QUIT command may be issued at any point to terminate the control connection.
After an authentication failure (e.g., 530 response to PASS), the client must re-issue a USER command to retry.

recommended_actions: Several observed message types are not yet represented in the state machine; prioritize seeds and probes for: ACCT, APPE, CDUP, DELE, EPRT, EPSV, FEAT, HELP

suggested_campaign.objective: Increase post-auth and data-channel coverage while validating weaker transitions.

status_changes.rationale: Standard initial authentication step evidenced by response code 331.
Successful password submission resulting in 230 response is well-evidenced.
Represents a failed login (530) remaining in an unauthenticated state; common in state machine modeling.
Negotiation of active mode (200) is a standard transition to ready state.
Standard transition when data channel is already established or defaults are used (150/226).
Standard file retrieval transition.
Implies the transfer state terminates and returns control to authenticated state upon subsequent command.
Implies return to authenticated state after transfer completion.
Standard session termination.
Standard multi-step rename initiation (350 code).
Completion of rename operation.
Standard but less frequent command; evidence identifies the behavior.
Logically follows session reinitialization, though trace specificities for REIN are less common.
Standard file upload initiation.
